Peter, President Biden and Congressional Democrats have made so much progress for working families – from overseeing record low unemployment to signing the largest-ever effort to curb the climate crisis.
We need to reelect President Biden in 2024 in order to keep building on this critical work. The problem is, the GOP is spreading misinformation about his record and doing everything they can to tarnish his reputation with voters.
**Recent polls show Donald Trump could really win this race, even with the ongoing and growing list of legal and criminal charges he's facing.**
**That's why Team Murray launched a poll to garner support for President Biden, and you only have a few hours to respond before our midnight deadline:**

name: Dr. Shawn Wilson for Governor
subject: Our first television ad
body:
image of ... [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/swlogo2.png]
TEAM WILSON WEEKLY UPDATES
Peter,
With a debate, forum, and now our first commercial televised [https://youtu.be/AXwApwpb13k?si=bEnXFZsBqKXU320h] over the last week, more and more Louisianans have learned that I am the best candidate to move Louisiana into the future. I’m the only person in this race with the experience delivering on critical issues and working with multiple administrations for the benefit of Louisianans across the state.
Together, we’ll make Louisiana safer, smarter, healthier, and wealthier. Election Day is October 14th and I’m asking all of you to turn out to VOTE!
https://youtu.be/AXwApwpb13k?si=bEnXFZsBqKXU320h [https://youtu.be/AXwApwpb13k?si=bEnXFZsBqKXU320h]
CHIP IN [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sw-web]
HIGHLIGHTS FROM THE CAMPAIGN TRAIL
Vernon Parish
#TeamWilson had an energizing evening in Central Louisiana with the Vernon Parish Democrats .
There was wonderful feedback from the first gubernatorial debate. According to the voters of Vernon Parish, we clearly stood out in the field of candidates.
wilsonforla.com [wilsonforla.com]
New Orleans
I had the opportunity to attend the Step Up Louisiana ‘N’ Geaux Vote Summit in New Orleans. We connected on education and economic transformation in our communities.
I’ll work every day to ensure that our kids are receiving world-class education in their schools, and that workers are paid with dignity!
wilsonforla.com [wilsonforla.com]
Baton Rouge
Last Saturday night football in Baton Rouge was a night to remember! We had three Louisiana teams playing Southern University vs. Jackson State & Grambling State University vs. LSU , and each fanbase was energized and ready to support their teams.
At each tailgate I had the opportunity to connect with fans and pitch my vision for Louisiana. If my vision resonated with you, head over to wilsonforla.com/take-action [wilsonforla.com/take-action] to get involved.
wilsonforla.com [wilsonforla.com]
New Orleans
I visited with Tunica-Biloxi and Southern University Law Center at their annual Rural Economic Development Summit . My plan to make Louisiana Safer, Smarter, Healthier, & Wealthier is perfectly aligned with their mission. As Governor we will partner with these established, solutions-focused entities.
In the audience was the matriarch of my wife Rocki’s family Aunt Bea, as well as Mrs. Rose Jackson, who taught her religion at Holy Family Catholic School in Marksville.
After my remarks, students from SUNO engaged in a good discussion about prison reform, and the importance of a unanimous jury.
wilsonforla.com [wilsonforla.com]
Port Allen
It was a pleasure to visit the Bridgeview Gun Club in Port Allen for the Jade Rung Clay Shoot Fundraiser . Louisiana is a sportsman's paradise and we have some of the best skeet shooters in the country.
As Governor, I will protect the rights of all Louisianans. Learn more at wilsonforla.com [wilsonforla.com] .
wilsonforla.com [wilsonforla.com]
New Orleans
Last Sunday, over 100 women came out to hear the Wilson Plan for Louisiana to make their families Safer, Smarter, Healthier, and Wealthier.
Want to get involved with Women For Wilson? Join today at wilsonforla.com/women [wilsonforla.com/women] .
wilsonforla.com [wilsonforla.com]
New Orleans
I was excited to be on hand for a big River District announcement . A new neighborhood is coming to New Orleans. Amazing opportunities are ahead to build on such a significant investment.
As Governor, I will proudly carry the baton of economic prosperity. Our state and the City of New Orleans is where people want to be, the last River Front land for development. Thank you Shell for anchoring in the 142,000 sq. ft. building.
wilsonforla.com [wilsonforla.com]
Baton Rouge
I was grateful for the chance to share my commitment to improving education at every level in our state at last night's gubernatorial forum at LSU - both before and during the event.
It was wonderful to spend some time before the forum connecting with students, hearing their concerns, and sharing my solutions.
Watch the forum at wilsonforla.com/lsuforum [wilsonforla.com/lsuforum]
wilsonforla.com [wilsonforla.com]
At the Louisiana Chemical Association & Louisiana Mid-Continent Oil & Gas Association (LMOGA) gubernatorial forum I highlighted my 25 years of bipartisan experience in state government.
We talked about ITEP, clean energy opportunities, unprecedented federal investments, education, and the workforce.
With my proven record of accomplishments, I am best prepared to lead on all these topics as Governor.
wilsonforla.com [wilsonforla.com]
West Bank
I was proud to be a West Banker this week for a forum at the University of Holy Cross hosted by AEDF, WBIA, PABI & GEDA .
Education, infrastructure, economic development, global trade, & Louisiana's insurance crisis were topics that I discussed with substance and solutions. AGAIN, it is leaders who show up.
The West Bank deserves a Governor who cares enough to be in the room.
wilsonforla.com [wilsonforla.com]
ENDORSEMENTS
Orleans Parish District Attorney Jason Williams
"Tough moments are coming and we need to make sure there's someone at the capitol who cares more about people over party."
Thank you to Orleans Parish District Attorney Jason Williams for the support and endorsement!
By working with everyone and putting people first, we can move Louisiana forward.
https://youtu.be/0RXn2iIAdWw?si=msV8JmiOpsXVKY7q [https://youtu.be/0RXn2iIAdWw?si=msV8JmiOpsXVKY7q]
Cedric Richmond
I am grateful to announce that Cedric Richmond has endorsed my candidacy for Louisiana Governor. Cedric has spent over 20 years directly representing the interests of the people of New Orleans and Louisiana as a Representative in the Louisiana House and then the US House of Representatives , where he served as Chair of the Congressional Black Caucus . I am honored to be endorsed by a leader who has been leading the charge advocating for Louisiana on the national stage - helping lead the President Biden transition team , serving as a senior advisor to the President as Director of Public Engagement and now working as a senior advisor to the Democratic National Committee .
"I have been fortunate enough to work with some of the most accomplished, effective leaders Louisiana and the United States have to offer, which includes Secretary Wilson. He has decades of receipts of good governance - providing the people of Louisiana with traffic relief and improvements in quality of life as Transportation Secretary. There are many wonderful things he can accomplish for Louisianans as Governor."
wilsoforla.com [wilsoforla.com]
Orleans Parish Clerks of Court
Orleans Parish’s trusted record keepers, the Clerks of Court , have joined the Wilson Wave. I'm proud to know that those whose role it is to ensure efficient governance know I will run my administration smoothly and effectively.
Thank you to Clerks of Court Donna Glapion , Chelsey Richard Napoleon , Darren Lombard , and Lisa Ray Diggs for your endorsement!
wilsoforla.com [wilsoforla.com]
Orleans Parish School Board
All the members of the Orleans Parish School Board agree, I am the best choice for Governor.
As the husband of an educator, I understand that an increased investment in our schools will improve outcomes in many areas critical for the advancement of Louisiana.
I am proud to be a champion of our educators and will work to get them the pay raises they deserve, and more.
wilsoforla.com [wilsoforla.com]
Greater Baton Rouge State Legislators
Greater Baton Rouge knows I will deliver for all Louisianans on what matters most.
I'm proud to announce that my campaign for Governor has been endorsed by State Senator Ed Price and State Representatives Edmond Jordan , Barbara West Carpenter , and Vanessa Caston LaFleur .
Let's keep the momentum going!
wilsoforla.com [wilsoforla.com]
Small Town Mayors
The Wilson Wave continues to swell as we get closer to Election Day. I am proud to announce that Mayors from every corner of our state are coming out in support of my candidacy for Governor.
From small towns to big cities, your trusted leaders trust me to deliver for all of Louisiana.
Let's geaux!
wilsoforla.com [wilsoforla.com]
New Orleans Coalition
The New Orleans Coalition 's mission to develop, sustain, and advance freedom, equality, democracy, and justice throughout the nation is one I wholly support. I am proud to have their endorsement.
wilsoforla.com [wilsoforla.com]
UPCOMING EVENTS
Join me Monday, September 18th , at the East Feliciana Minister's Conference candidate forum . I look forward to participating in their "Souls to the polls" effort as we rocket towards Election Day.
Early voting starts September 30th! Let's geaux!
wilsoforla.com [wilsoforla.com]
To learn more about my candidacy visit wilsonforla.com [wilsonforla.com] .
HOST SHAWN AT AN EVENT [events@wilsonforla.com?subject=Host%20Shawn%20At%20An%20Event]
JOIN WOMEN FOR WILSON [wilsonforla.com/women]
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sw-web]
Hope to see you soon!
Shawn Wilson
https://www.facebook.com/ [https://www.facebook.com/] https://twitter.com/ [https://twitter.com/] https://instagram.com/ [https://instagram.com/] https://www.linkedin.com/ [https://www.linkedin.com/]

Wilson for Louisiana
P.O. Box 4572
Baton Rouge, LA 70821
United States
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/408Tlfh_cUKqKl0Yd-tKBw2?unsubscribedata=x%2BJ4XcQ%2FH1znVUtkwt7Ctwk4O%2F5i4XdDni9zzIwnIN9lztwhiPVoM2aypku%2BPlM1F8GhvWIzj33TDVe55ChrIFv9L1U3Os1xh71c%2Bb%2BKib7P66%2F2W5bmaY1N6fbxhw7BVXFrKa5IxiSNitNwqUGK244ogbnUFGBU9mHG47Ipzke39WAQa6vg6EckOTRk%2FooU%2F6CGiyTpgr5FBntWOmSEH2PN5DrtOqrlIoH%2Fazxki5Kti5j0KTiTKZWfLkbbGDfyvLm7Wh%2F6vvhqcNTHIh0MxGpwwb7dgAH1fUXPhzdadi1slB0AsIuzr2rCBSSP%2F5nulBZKy7KGJJh1xaMdy0Iof2oIEVaywDCk7UroULaqpok8LgyECgqwsGg7L%2FXZ0oZPfi%2BZ2mEH0EsneEZkhxFOwuUh5e6o08PoOrz7q13qgfSlBabUpF2OH2QzH8r%2FF8Do .

name: Team Padora
subject: Sunday Funday
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Padora for Colorado
body:
Happy Sunday, folks! We hope that you’re kicking back and enjoying the last day of the weekend! Now that it’s not quite as hot out, and Broncos football is back, we’re closing in on the 1-year out mark for the 2024 election. | | | [](http://links.padoraforcongress.com/u/click?_t=1632d2fd715245289db23a5b99e588cb&_m=a92ad5cd6ca7498dba615abb7af69ddd&_e=KZKz2VBtqm6VprQ9Asf6u-tJZ4JM1OBNTEtQIo9Ma5AnLgN7X8UgewOIyX_FV5Qckw8ensntTNyNYaEp1KTu9SRv19ThQ723f2UzGKUlvuRwVKbGyyol8CD-dst_Kq2S1hCj_6g-Bog3_QO5fbTtDy_I5Xc4xXrDysZDiFaYP__Gsat0F6CY6RdLrbcQdOxp6yDKDr3weOeyvDlaRd5d0dN2tBFUbE8qUrCTYiwJT3qKToUeA7qWNIYOAnesOtz8_14Qjd7_EXcxVMoERgQp8DjHOyb6JwXey0Gm5R_rmlq4-4iRfyuaefdh7EU_638-oGw3pdaAwuN7A8GCzvmW_NacawHFsKDCjhIyIu4jeclCwEZweSmYgxoAl8N26m6EYhKH5epkGueRaPzCLvIVMg%3D%3D) Happy Sunday, folks! We hope that you’re kicking back and enjoying the last day of the weekend! Now that it’s not quite as hot out, and Broncos football is back, we’re closing in on the 1-year out mark for the 2024 election. We told you that Election Day would be here before you know it, and now that Labor Day has passed, it’s really time to kick things into gear. John is going to be all over the district for the next year going to events, knocking on doors, and winning every vote possible. But while he’s doing that, he will be relying on us to keep building a campaign that can defeat Ken Buck. **[So before Sunday comes to an end, and the scaries for next week kick in, will you make a donation of $5 or $10 to John’s **My story is not unique.** I was born and raised in Pennsylvania in rural, blue-collar, and old coal mining communities. I'm a proud husband and father to three amazing children. I witnessed firsthand how both major political parties have neglected rural communities and left behind working families.
I'm running to build a movement and a government that works for working families. name: Team Kennedy
subject: From the bottom of our hearts, thank you!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TEAM KENNEDY
body:
This year, Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. called on all of us to come together and address the needs in our local communities by organizing service projects for the September 11th National Days of Service and Remembrance.

| [ ](https://www.kennedy24.com/?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=september_11th_non_donor_1&n=1)
---
Dear Peter, This year, Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. called on all of us to come together and address the needs in our local communities by organizing service projects for the September 11th National Days of Service and Remembrance. We are truly speechless by how many of you answered that call. Thousands of volunteers participated in over 225 service projects in 44 states and Washington, D.C., in communities across America. On behalf of Team Kennedy: Thank you for your extraordinary service. [](https://www.kennedy24.com/thank_you_for_your_service?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=september_11th_non_donor_1&n=2) | [](https://www.kennedy24.com/thank_you_for_your_service?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=september_11th_non_donor_1&n=3 "Watch the Video") [Watch the Video](https://www.kennedy24.com/thank_you_for_your_service?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=september_11th_non_donor_1&n=3)
---
We are so inspired by your creativity and how you designed projects to meet the specific needs of your communities.
In Mobile, Alabama, a group gathered to provide haircuts to the homeless. In Hawaii, a former Peace Corps volunteer organized a ukulele “Kanikapila”, a family jam session, in a nursing home. In South Dakota, volunteers did groundskeeping work for a Veterans and first responders retreat center focused on healing PTSD.
In Utah, volunteers taught foster children preparing to transition to independent living how to cook healthy meals affordably. In Maryland, families washed fire trucks and EMS vehicles. Volunteers joined home building projects in South Carolina and Vermont, and others worked on home repair projects for neighbors in need in Texas.
_“It felt refreshingly exhilarating to actually do SOMETHING to make a difference in our city.” - Volunteer Sally Rubin_
So many projects focused on the environment with dozens of beach, river, lake, canal, trail, park and street cleanups generating hundreds of bags of trash from the Skagit River in Mount Vernon, Washington, to the Mississippi River in St. Louis, Missouri, and the streets near a homeless shelter under the elevated subway in New York City.
Many projects focused on food access at urban farms, faith based groups, food banks, homeless shelters, and more.
Other projects focused on peace and healing by incorporating meditation, yoga, arts and crafts, healthy food for those with chronic illness, and education outreach for individuals on Skid Row and in the Tenderloin districts of California.
Mr. Kennedy participated in a service project at Abenaki Springs Organic Farm in Walpole, New Hampshire, which is still recovering from a flood several months ago. He worked with a volunteer group of over 20 people to pull weeds in the organic cauliflower patch. Together, the volunteers achieved what would normally take the farmers weeks, in just a few hours.
When speaking with the volunteers, after a moment of silence and prayer in remembrance of the lives lost on and since September 11, 2001, Mr. Kennedy shared his own experience of loss and tragedy on that day and in the aftermath, participating in the rescue and recovery at Ground Zero.
_“Honoring the Soul's lost on 9/11 by the creek was powerful, setting an intention to breathe peace, love and light into their memory, rather than memorialize the traumatic sadness of their passing.” - Volunteer Bren Logan_
He talked about how we process grief and try to take the chaos and make some kind of order, reminding us of the unprecedented unity that occurred in our country in the days following the September 11th, 2001. He also reminded us of how this moment of unity in our nation was hijacked and used for war, bringing out the darker side of our nature instead of the brighter side.
Mr. Kennedy advised us to remember both of those lessons, the aspirational lesson of how we ought to be in our country—how we ought to care for each other, be in touch with our soils and the earth, grow wholesome foods to keep our children from getting sick, and build sustainable communities. At the same time, we must be vigilant about the capacity for powerful interests to use national tragedies to manipulate us away from our highest values.
He said, “I’m running for this office because I want to underscore that aspirational reason of what happened after 9/11 toward unity in our country and the unity around the highest ideals of our country.”
This Day of Service was just the beginning of a much larger movement of healing and change for our country. Many of you have expressed to us that you plan to continue the work of coming together to serve your community, and we want to encourage you to keep this spirit of service, and community-building, going.
We, at Team Kennedy, want to thank all of you, hosts and volunteers, because you are what made this initiative a great success, and you are responsible for this movement. We also want to thank Mr. Kennedy for inspiring us and planting this seed, so that we can grow to be a healthy and whole country again.
Warm Regards,
Team Kennedy
[Dayofservice@teamkennedy.com](mailto:Dayofservice@teamkennedy.com)
[](https://www.kennedy24.com/?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=september_11th_non_donor_1&n=4)[https://www.kennedy24.com/](https://www.kennedy24.com/?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=september_11th_non_donor_1&n=5)
[Donate today](https://www.kennedy24.com/donate?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=september_11th_non_donor_1&n=6) to put a Kennedy back in the White House — Elect Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. as President to unite America in 2024! Make sure to join us on Social Media to help spread our campaign's message and put Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. in the White House! [ ](https://www.kennedy24.com/r?u=27Ax0UmWOX2J8J_QwK9Z6nQ2OSzJvbzn8H14UVJyZeU&e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=september_11th_non_donor_1&n=7) [ ](https://www.kennedy24.com/r?u=YqgukYA3Mrlri-dqG7I8TvJMDEmDChn4crVEdym-uYEvPdkFBV9SaYXx39MwIdLI&e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=september_11th_non_donor_1&n=8) [ ](https://www.kennedy24.com/r?u=6hyvAYXBs1PELL28GD73y2F3NBqF5BJJJ4dLoTp9j7umB4CtvDL9QcHxwZkP_0TO&e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=september_11th_non_donor_1&n=9)
* * *

Make sure to join us on Social Media to help spread our campaign's message and put Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. in the White House! * PAID FOR BY TEAM KENNEDY

I never envisioned myself running for Congress.
But in 2017, when I saw all the hateful things far-right extremists were saying about people like my mother — who is a proud Mexican immigrant, single mom, and hard worker — I knew New Mexico needed someone who understood our values. We needed someone to step up and fight for us.
Our community deserves better. I love every part of this district, the unique culture and history. And I’ve been to every corner of it! Your support is how I pulled this off the first time.
It wasn’t easy. I wasn’t hand-picked by the establishment, and **the folks in Washington said there was no way we could flip this seat blue.** But every day I woke up determined to show my community they had a choice in their future. **I won with less than one percent of the vote.**
I know it’s going to be even harder this time around. **I’m in a rematch against a far-right extremist who voted against taking away women’s choice and slashing funding for New Mexico.** Kevin McCarthy is angry that we flipped this seat last year, and he’s determined to spend as much money as necessary to win it back simply because there’s a (D) behind my name.
That’s where you come in. Your voice matters. The best way to fight back against these right-wing extremists is to show them that our people-powered campaign is stronger than ever — especially in these first critical days.
**[Can I count on you to become a Founding Donor to my re-election campaign by splitting a donation of $10 between my campaign and Martin Heinrich today? What happens in the next few days will determine the tone of our entire campaign, and I need you with me.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69907637/427666657/-1762724559?amount=10&refcode=AU_MTH_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20230917_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NDJhZDdmYy1iNzU1LWVlMTEtOTkzNy0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1YVMUCHanw3E-DLn2BbpEZ5kHprV7sfWshqHh2qONTg=)**
Thanks so much for your support. I’ll be in touch soon with more details about my plans for 2024 and beyond.
— Gabe
| Paid for by Martin Heinrich for Senate
---
Senator Martin Heinrich is committed to helping New Mexico become a leader in defense, tech, and energy with opportunities in every corner. # [They call that news?](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=137123378&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTM3MTIzMzc4LCJpYXQiOjE2OTQ5OTc1MDQsImV4cCI6MTY5NzU4OTUwNCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.CAMeazo9E262Ey5KzGSkr0PK2cmgtbT8nMGXX5LP3Wc)
### Yet again, journalists & editors from DC & New York to San Francisco & Los Angeles have been duped--forcing voters to pay the price
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/redirect/45ee9b42-2748-40a0-9056-982cf396ee03?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| Sep 18| | | ∙| | Preview
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/45ee9b42-2748-40a0-9056-982cf396ee03?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=137123378&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTM3MTIzMzc4LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTY5NDk5NzUwNCwiZXhwIjoxNjk3NTg5NTA0LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.RxtNIC9QgH0NGHFysESG_7CGSoiXXRWnKOh5snS9oHw&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=137123378&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTM3MTIzMzc4LCJpYXQiOjE2OTQ5OTc1MDQsImV4cCI6MTY5NzU4OTUwNCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.CAMeazo9E262Ey5KzGSkr0PK2cmgtbT8nMGXX5LP3Wc&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=137123378&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTM3MTIzMzc4LCJpYXQiOjE2OTQ5OTc1MDQsImV4cCI6MTY5NzU4OTUwNCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.CAMeazo9E262Ey5KzGSkr0PK2cmgtbT8nMGXX5LP3Wc)

| | [](https://substack..Tryc4K1atgSDK_PrgvohP03JV4CNdVPKOBEZvWgNv7o?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/shahidbuttar/p/they-call-that-news?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store)
---
Last week, former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi announced that she would be seeking reelection to represent San Francisco in the House of Representatives for her 36th and 37th years. She appeared on MSNBC in a [fawning interview](https://substack.com/redirect/99f5be17-4b07-4ab7-9640-fcb008d52005?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that both ignored her record entirely, and allowed her to continue spreading any number of falsehoods without fact checking.
While that may seem disappointing, the lack of independence reflects MSNBC's role as a propaganda outlet supporting the corporate Democratic Party. More broadly, the news cycle responding to Pelosi's reelection announcement reflects how widely the press has been co-opted, from corporate outlets like MSNBC and the _Los Angeles Times_ to supposedly nonprofit independent outlets like _48 Hills_ and the _San Francisco Examiner_.
I offer my thoughts to empower grassroots advocates, and rely on readers to share them with others. This post is public, so feel free to share it with your friends!
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=137123378&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTM3MTIzMzc4LCJpYXQiOjE2OTQ5OTc1MDQsImV4cCI6MTY5NzU4OTUwNCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.CAMeazo9E262Ey5KzGSkr0PK2cmgtbT8nMGXX5LP3Wc)
### Pelosi meets the press
Pelosi's interview with MSNBC reflects several fallacies widely embraced by the press. She claims that San Francisco needs her in Washington to help address the [various](https://substack.com/redirect/f06151af-4c80-46d5-bfb6-46b49d65b68c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [sources](https://substack.com/redirect/afb49f23-9524-4253-aae9-cb10f0e01957?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of [dysfunction](https://substack.com/redirect/07bba889-6957-4d47-9b1b-d03f28fcce6f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that have come to plague the city, as if she herself did not contrive them through 35 years of shamelessly representing capital rather than her constituents.
One of San Francisco's greatest challenges is homelessness, which Pelosi has actively fueled by repeatedly favoring the wealthy over working people in her legislation. From recurring tax cuts for the rich, to blocking crucially needed programs like universal healthcare, Pelosi has been not only an active agent--but also a self-interested one, as a person born to [inherited wealth](https://substack.com/redirect/6b8e93e0-5cab-42fc-a0d5-bd9147957b45?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [privilege](https://substack.com/redirect/aa9d7d42-0fe4-4895-b85a-791903a491d2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--in the federal government's continuing programs to transfer wealth upward from working people to oligarchs and robber barons.
Pelosi also described a need to defend democracy--as if she had not evaded public debates for two generations, while leading a party that continues to protect entrenched incumbents (like herself) by smearing challengers more committed to human rights and the needs of our communities, and filing lawsuits to block third-party candidates from fairly appearing on the ballot. She and her party rely on corporate support while falsely claiming to represent the people.
### Reality rather than rhetoric
In the wake of Pelosi's reelection announcement, I had the opportunity to rejoin Sabrina Salvati (aka Sabby Sabs) from the Revolutionary Black Network to share my experience running against Pelosi and explore some of the various failures repeated by media outlets from MSNBC and the _LA Times_ to the _San Francisco Chronicle_ and _SF Examiner_.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/575ae1e4-baa0-4463-90bc-2883e9ea9130?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
At root, the central failing of media outlets covering Pelosi's reelection announcement is that they treated it as news at all, by previously indulging rumors planted by her supporters that she was planning to retire.
At multiple points in the past, news outlets have treated announcements from Pelosi office as if she plans to step down from her seat, which she has never promised to do even once. She [promised to step down as Speaker of the House in 2017](https://substack.com/redirect/6beb542f-9cf6-4fe8-91fe-1f8c69234097?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), only to break that promise without anyone in the press particularly noticing.
My efforts to promote accountability cost me my career, but I'm committed to speaking the truth. To receive new posts and support my work, please subscribe--whether on a free or paid basis.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..IV0C-9YH12M0xAo8BICXqAMz_pMdsSGrwI5F16Wy3Bo?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=137123378)
In a functioning democracy, running for office would convey an implicit willingness to appear before the public, answer questiond, and defend ideas, policy preferences and goals. That's the only way for voters to know how a candidate anticipates representing their district during their term in office.
Yet Nancy Pelosi has not appeared in a public debate since 1987--and her record reflects a litany of issues where her votes in Congress and political choices have revealed her public comments to be something between misrepresentations and outright (often self-serving) lies.
Pelosi claims to care about reproductive freedom, despite having openly [supported](https://substack.com/redirect/bac4633e-e430-4e0a-853a-44bf4114b1a0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) anti-choice Democrats against progressive women of color.
Pelosi claims to care about working families, while bending over backwards to [deny them a right to healthcare](https://substack.com/redirect/b8c5ed2f-53e9-4675-9c6a-4eb80b2fd770?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and supporting reductions in their benefits that have increased the poverty level to its [highest point](https://substack.com/redirect/1a671b0c-aecd-4e6a-af50-0fcdce978cf8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in decades.
Pelosi claims to care about human rights, despite having chosen to fund concentration camps for migrants with [no protections for human rights](https://substack.com/redirect/eba12625-0d16-4a56-b649-731b663111f7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) guaranteed under international law.
Pelosi claims to care about democracy, while ducking debates for 35 years, unconstitutionally using her office to fill her own pockets through illegal insider trading, and leading a party that maintains its grip on power by leveraging racist lies and disinformation to protect entrenched legislators like herself whose records can't withstand public scrutiny.
To me, one of the most offensive parts of her interview on MSNBC is when Pelosi bragged about her time leading the House Intelligence Committee. The committee was created in the 70s to oversee national security agencies after Congress caught them [illegally spying](https://substack.com/redirect/cedb9c45-0f1d-40f9-a0a3-0829ca1791cf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on Americans and plotting to kill civil rights leaders.
It was under Pelosi's watch that the committees became thoroughly co-opted. She has spent 35 years in Washington as a cheerleader for illegal wars, mass surveillance, and impunity for human rights violations including torture.
As a representative of a city that claims progressive values who has come to command immense influence in Washington, Pelosi's role as a leader of the Democratic Party is akin to that of a Trojan horse.
When I ran for office, I observed any number of instances where Pelosi's work advanced Republican policy priorities--yet writers & editors refused to report either on the facts, or the alternative presented by the only intraparty general election opponent she has ever faced. Ultimately, more lies were published about my campaign than anything resembling the truth, while Pelosi gained the benefit of ultimately false rumors that she would retire if left her own devices.
I explained in real time that those rumors were untrue. Yet journalists and writers have better things to do than investigate facts, and publish the truth, like publish puff pieces to create cults of personality and mislead voters into undermining their own interests.
In 2022, the _Los Angeles Times_ published an article envisioning the future of Pelosi's seat, as if she were not facing a historic challenge at the time. While imagining whether her daughter or a state Senator would be more likely to replace her, the _Times_ ignored the election that was actually happening at the time, simply imagining a future transition of power that will likely never happen.
In addition to [articles presented as news reports](https://substack.com/redirect/74085a4e-ffe4-46a1-a678-41f4d1fdb668?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by the _San Francisco Examiner_ , a column by Columbia University's Lincoln Mitchell [did the very same thing](https://substack.com/redirect/2b7e38ec-8e80-4c57-aae1-f783efd64e5c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Mitchell explored whether Christian Pelosi would be the most likely voice to replace her mother in Congress after she chose to retire--even though she was not running for office, and her mother was not retiring. At the time it was written, the primary was four months away. I ultimately missed the crucial second position, which would have enabled me to face Pelosi in November a second time, by 600 votes.
It's especially fascinating to see reelection announcements by longserving incumbents lead to multiple appearances on national media outlets. One of the factors driving the astronomical reelection rate in Congress, despite public disappointment in Congress as a whole, is the unique access that incumbents enjoy to the public. The franking privilege is one such example that allows them to send mail for free through the U.S. Postal Service. This discriminatory receptivity of the press plays a similar--and ultimately, even more influential--role.
It's worth noting that this is not only a problem with corporate media, but also with outlets that present themselves publicly as supposedly "nonprofit," or "independent."
One of the worst offenders of press ethics in coverage addressing Pelosi is _48 Hills_ , a nominally nonprofit, supposedly independent local outlet with a long history of publishing lies constructed to kneecap black and brown candidates. Editor Tim Redmond published stories--like those in the _LA Times_ --imagining the future of Pelosi's seat while ignoring candidates who were actually running, [presuming Pelosi's retirement](https://substack.com/redirect/9b7357af-a59e-4097-bb02-691ad0ee1ecc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) despite her continuing refusal to step aside, and promoting establishment voices who lacked the independence to actually throw their hats in the ring.
Redmond uniquely went beyond that layer of disinformation. First, he gleefully [smeared me](https://substack.com/redirect/924dbdbb-ae96-4daa-b2ef-fd4403c53f05?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), like many other writers in the city and across the country, when I was predictably falsely accused while presenting the only alternative that San Francisco has had in nearly 40 years. Redmond then went further than all of the other outlets complicit in my character assassination by declaring that I was "unqualified" to serve in Congress, despite 20 years of advocacy at the national level, teaching at an elite law school, and influencing policies from Washington to Sacramento.
My favorite part about Redmond's mendacity is his [public admission](https://substack.com/redirect/ab5e09b4-208a-4e56-8dd4-39b798e47c2e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that he knows nothing about the national and international policy arenas in which I grounded my career for 20 years before running for Congress. He seemed to imagine that a presence in his corner of the city was a qualification to serve in Congress, ignoring the decades I spent fighting Pelosi's policy agenda in Washington as a grassroots activist and non-profit leader working far beyond his neighborhood.
### Will they ever get it right?
Beyond Pelosi's recent announcement and predictable re-election, the 2024 election cycle appears to be lining up as [a predictable repeat of previous](https://substack.com/redirect/1b7bc843-e323-4c26-b9a2-444f16b84a8c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) contests in which the will of the people find itself overwhelmed by the preferences of capital.
That was the story in 2016 and 2020, when the leading Democratic nominee for the presidency was [kneecapped by the party establishment](https://substack.com/redirect/e51ea404-b56d-4822-84e6-68a30f5cf50e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in favor of voices long aligned with Wall Street. While Biden was able to overcome Trump in 2020, there is no reason to believe that he will be able to do so again.
Meanwhile, the press is yet again falling into the trap of [constructing a cult of personality](https://substack.com/redirect/97e81a99-9f7e-4e9d-9a30-60e438727460?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) surrounding the former president, despite his open distain for democracy and working Americans.
In this moment, a free, independent, and robust press establishment would be one of the most crucial levers We the People might enjoy to defend our democracy. The critical role for transparency and accountability is reflected in the First Amendment, which grants the press particular privileges precisely because the public needs to know what is happening in Washington.
Yet, despite a presidential contest looming in just over a year, most national media outlets have never mentioned the names of Biden's most prominent challengers, Democrats Marianne Williamson and Robert F. Kennedy, Jr, and [Cornel West](https://substack.com/redirect/882fb0da-8da0-4a14-9ad0-11f931d40886?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from the Green Party...

clean_body: Yet again, journalists & editors from DC & New York to San Francisco & Los Angeles have been duped--forcing voters to pay the price | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Yet again, journalists & editors from DC & New York to San Francisco & Los Angeles have been duped--forcing voters to pay the price | | | Sep 18| | | ∙| | Preview ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | Last week, former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi announced that she would be seeking reelection to represent San Francisco in the House of Representatives for her 36th and 37th years. She appeared on MSNBC in a that both ignored her record entirely, and allowed her to continue spreading any number of falsehoods without fact checking. While that may seem disappointing, the lack of independence reflects MSNBC's role as a propaganda outlet supporting the corporate Democratic Party. More broadly, the news cycle responding to Pelosi's reelection announcement reflects how widely the press has been co-opted, from corporate outlets like MSNBC and the Los Angeles Times to supposedly nonprofit independent outlets like 48 Hills and the San Francisco Examiner. I offer my thoughts to empower grassroots advocates, and rely on readers to share them with others. This post is public, so feel free to share it with your friends! Pelosi meets the press Pelosi's interview with MSNBC reflects several fallacies widely embraced by the press. She claims that San Francisco needs her in Washington to help address the of that have come to plague the city, as if she herself did not contrive them through 35 years of shamelessly representing capital rather than her constituents. One of San Francisco's greatest challenges is homelessness, which Pelosi has actively fueled by repeatedly favoring the wealthy over working people in her legislation. From recurring tax cuts for the rich, to blocking crucially needed programs like universal healthcare, Pelosi has been not only an active agent--but also a self-interested one, as a person born to and the federal government's continuing programs to transfer wealth upward from working people to oligarchs and robber barons. Pelosi also described a need to defend democracy--as if she had not evaded public debates for two generations, while leading a party that continues to protect entrenched incumbents (like herself) by smearing challengers more committed to human rights and the needs of our communities, and filing lawsuits to block third-party candidates from fairly appearing on the ballot. She and her party rely on corporate support while falsely claiming to represent the people. Reality rather than rhetoric In the wake of Pelosi's reelection announcement, I had the opportunity to rejoin Sabrina Salvati (aka Sabby Sabs) from the Revolutionary Black Network to share my experience running against Pelosi and explore some of the various failures repeated by media outlets from MSNBC and the LA Times to the San Francisco Chronicle and SF Examiner. At root, the central failing of media outlets covering Pelosi's reelection announcement is that they treated it as news at all, by previously indulging rumors planted by her supporters that she was planning to retire. At multiple points in the past, news outlets have treated announcements from Pelosi office as if she plans to step down from her seat, which she has never promised to do even once. She , only to break that promise without anyone in the press particularly noticing. My efforts to promote accountability cost me my career, but I'm committed to speaking the truth. To receive new posts and support my work, please subscribe--whether on a free or paid basis. In a functioning democracy, running for office would convey an implicit willingness to appear before the public, answer questiond, and defend ideas, policy preferences and goals. That's the only way for voters to know how a candidate anticipates representing their district during their term in office. Yet Nancy Pelosi has not appeared in a public debate since 1987--and her record reflects a litany of issues where her votes in Congress and political choices have revealed her public comments to be something between misrepresentations and outright (often self-serving) lies. Pelosi claims to care about reproductive freedom, despite having openly anti-choice Democrats against progressive women of color. Pelosi claims to care about working families, while bending over backwards to and supporting reductions in their benefits that have increased the poverty level to its in decades. Pelosi claims to care about human rights, despite having chosen to fund concentration camps for migrants with guaranteed under international law. Pelosi claims to care about democracy, while ducking debates for 35 years, unconstitutionally using her office to fill her own pockets through illegal insider trading, and leading a party that maintains its grip on power by leveraging racist lies and disinformation to protect entrenched legislators like herself whose records can't withstand public scrutiny. To me, one of the most offensive parts of her interview on MSNBC is when Pelosi bragged about her time leading the House Intelligence Committee. The committee was created in the 70s to oversee national security agencies after Congress caught them on Americans and plotting to kill civil rights leaders. It was under Pelosi's watch that the committees became thoroughly co-opted. She has spent 35 years in Washington as a cheerleader for illegal wars, mass surveillance, and impunity for human rights violations including torture. As a representative of a city that claims progressive values who has come to command immense influence in Washington, Pelosi's role as a leader of the Democratic Party is akin to that of a Trojan horse. When I ran for office, I observed any number of instances where Pelosi's work advanced Republican policy priorities--yet writers & editors refused to report either on the facts, or the alternative presented by the only intraparty general election opponent she has ever faced. Ultimately, more lies were published about my campaign than anything resembling the truth, while Pelosi gained the benefit of ultimately false rumors that she would retire if left her own devices. I explained in real time that those rumors were untrue. Yet journalists and writers have better things to do than investigate facts, and publish the truth, like publish puff pieces to create cults of personality and mislead voters into undermining their own interests. In 2022, the Los Angeles Times published an article envisioning the future of Pelosi's seat, as if she were not facing a historic challenge at the time. While imagining whether her daughter or a state Senator would be more likely to replace her, the Times ignored the election that was actually happening at the time, simply imagining a future transition of power that will likely never happen. In addition to by the San Francisco Examiner , a column by Columbia University's Lincoln Mitchell . Mitchell explored whether Christian Pelosi would be the most likely voice to replace her mother in Congress after she chose to retire--even though she was not running for office, and her mother was not retiring. At the time it was written, the primary was four months away. I ultimately missed the crucial second position, which would have enabled me to face Pelosi in November a second time, by 600 votes. It's especially fascinating to see reelection announcements by longserving incumbents lead to multiple appearances on national media outlets. One of the factors driving the astronomical reelection rate in Congress, despite public disappointment in Congress as a whole, is the unique access that incumbents enjoy to the public. The franking privilege is one such example that allows them to send mail for free through the U.S. Postal Service. This discriminatory receptivity of the press plays a similar--and ultimately, even more influential--role. It's worth noting that this is not only a problem with corporate media, but also with outlets that present themselves publicly as supposedly "nonprofit," or "independent." One of the worst offenders of press ethics in coverage addressing Pelosi is 48 Hills , a nominally nonprofit, supposedly independent local outlet with a long history of publishing lies constructed to kneecap black and brown candidates. Editor Tim Redmond published stories--like those in the LA Times --imagining the future of Pelosi's seat while ignoring candidates who were actually running, despite her continuing refusal to step aside, and promoting establishment voices who lacked the independence to actually throw their hats in the ring. Redmond uniquely went beyond that layer of disinformation. First, he gleefully , like many other writers in the city and across the country, when I was predictably falsely accused while presenting the only alternative that San Francisco has had in nearly 40 years. Redmond then went further than all of the other outlets complicit in my character assassination by declaring that I was "unqualified" to serve in Congress, despite 20 years of advocacy at the national level, teaching at an elite law school, and influencing policies from Washington to Sacramento. My favorite part about Redmond's mendacity is his that he knows nothing about the national and international policy arenas in which I grounded my career for 20 years before running for Congress. He seemed to imagine that a presence in his corner of the city was a qualification to serve in Congress, ignoring the decades I spent fighting Pelosi's policy agenda in Washington as a grassroots activist and non-profit leader working far beyond his neighborhood. Will they ever get it right? Beyond Pelosi's recent announcement and predictable re-election, the 2024 election cycle appears to be lining up as contests in which the will of the people find itself overwhelmed by the preferences of capital. That was the story in 2016 and 2020, when the leading Democratic nominee for the presidency was in favor of voices long aligned with Wall Street. While Biden was able to overcome Trump in 2020, there is no reason to believe that he will be able to do so again. Meanwhile, the press is yet again falling into the trap of surrounding the former president, despite his open distain for democracy and working Americans. In this moment, a free, independent, and robust press establishment would be one of the most crucial levers We the People might enjoy to defend our democracy. The critical role for transparency and accountability is reflected in the First Amendment, which grants the press particular privileges precisely because the public needs to know what is happening in Washington. Yet, despite a presidential contest looming in just over a year, most national media outlets have never mentioned the names of Biden's most prominent challengers, Democrats Marianne Williamson and Robert F. name: Angela Alsobrooks
subject: A little about me!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
| [ ](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/52866?t=6&akid=10471%2E878179%2EtCei2c) Hi folks, Angela Alsobrooks here!

Our team is growing every single day, so I’d like to take a moment to introduce (or re-introduce) myself and remind folks of what this campaign is all about!

Here’s a little about me: I’m a 26-year public servant, former State’s Attorney, County Executive of Prince George’s County, and I’m running to become the next United States Senator from Maryland!

I’m also a born-and-raised Marylander, mom to a first-year college student, a Beyhive member, and a breakdancer!

[**There’s more, but before I tell you all about why I decided to run, will you chip in a split donation of $10 to help power my campaign for the U.S. Senate and support the Voter Protection Project? Our September FEC End-of-Quarter deadline is quickly approaching, so this is an all-hands-on-deck moment!**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/52866?t=7&akid=10471%2E878179%2EtCei2c) If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Angela Alsobrooks and VPP will go through immediately: | [CONTRIBUTE $10 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/52867?t=8&akid=10471%2E878179%2EtCei2c)
---
[CONTRIBUTE $25 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/52868?t=9&akid=10471%2E878179%2EtCei2c)
[CONTRIBUTE $50 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/52869?t=10&akid=10471%2E878179%2EtCei2c)
[CONTRIBUTE $100 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/52870?t=11&akid=10471%2E878179%2EtCei2c)
[Or a custom donation »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/52866?t=12&akid=10471%2E878179%2EtCei2c)
I believe that our leaders should share the values and experiences of the people they represent.

I don’t live like or think like most Senators. I know what life is like for the average Marylander, because I’ve lived it. I see the challenges we face, and I know we can overcome them together.

That outlook is a family heirloom handed down to me by my great-grandmother, Maebel James — the strongest lady I’ve ever met. She was unflinching in the face of so much cruelty and injustice, and she had one rule for her family: **No complaining.

She’d tell all her grandkids and great-grandkids, _“If you don't like it, you go farther and you do better.”_**

The example set by my great-grandmother and by my parents led me to enter public service almost three decades ago.

I worked to modernize our criminal justice system, develop economic opportunities, protect the most vulnerable people in our communities, build prosperity for working families, and strengthen education.

We went farther and did better in this campaign for Senate — with your help, we set a state-wide first quarter fundraising record!

**I’m so, so proud of everything we’ve accomplished so far, and there’s plenty of work to do in the Senate. With a little determination and a good attitude, we’ll keep going farther and doing better for all Marylanders.**

[**Friend, if you can, will you chip in $10 split between the Voter Protection Project and my campaign to help us knock our FEC End-of-Quarter fundraising out of the park? I can’t tell you just how much I value your support.**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/52866?t=13&akid=10471%2E878179%2EtCei2c)
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Angela Alsobrooks and VPP will go through immediately:
[CONTRIBUTE $10 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/52867?t=14&akid=10471%2E878179%2EtCei2c)
---
[CONTRIBUTE $25 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/52868?t=15&akid=10471%2E878179%2EtCei2c)
[CONTRIBUTE $50 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/52869?t=16&akid=10471%2E878179%2EtCei2c)
[CONTRIBUTE $100 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/52870?t=17&akid=10471%2E878179%2EtCei2c)
[Or a custom donation »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/52866?t=18&akid=10471%2E878179%2EtCei2c)
With love and gratitude,

Angela Alsobrooks
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=10471%2E878179%2EtCei2c). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=10471%2E878179%2EtCei2c).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=10471%2E878179%2EtCei2c) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=19&akid=10471%2E878179%2EtCei2c). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=10471%2E878179%2EtCei2c)
---
[Donate Just One Time »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/124?t=5&akid=10471%2E878179%2EtCei2c)

Hello,
It’s hard to explain what it feels like to turn on the TV or get on social media and see Lauren Boebert attacking your dad with lie after lie.
Before Dad decided to run against Boebert in 2022, my parents tried to warn my sister and me what would happen. It’s one thing to imagine what it’ll feel like; it’s another to experience it. Especially when the attacks got really, really nasty.
My dad, Adam Frisch, barely lost last year to Lauren Boebert. It was just 546 votes. I really thought he might pull it off when things went to a recount. But unfortunately, we lost in the closest race in the country.
Now, Dad’s running again – and I _know_ he’s going to win in 2024. But the attacks from Boebert and her allies have already started...
His campaign has set a goal of raising another $100,000 before midnight tonight, and I wanted to send an email to see if you could chip in to help Dad defeat Lauren Boebert.
[**It means a lot to our entire family that people like you have got his back. clean_body: It’s hard to explain what it feels like to turn on the TV or get on social media and see Lauren Boebert attacking your dad with lie after lie. Hello, It’s hard to explain what it feels like to turn on the TV or get on social media and see Lauren Boebert attacking your dad with lie after lie. Before Dad decided to run against Boebert in 2022, my parents tried to warn my sister and me what would happen. It’s one thing to imagine what it’ll feel like; it’s another to experience it. Especially when the attacks got really, really nasty. My dad, Adam Frisch, barely lost last year to Lauren Boebert. It was just 546 votes. I really thought he might pull it off when things went to a recount. But unfortunately, we lost in the closest race in the country. Now, Dad’s running again – and I know he’s going to win in 2024. But the attacks from Boebert and her allies have already started... His campaign has set a goal of raising another $100,000 before midnight tonight, and I wanted to send an email to see if you could chip in to help Dad defeat Lauren Boebert. = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = Dad and I have already started driving our pickup truck around the district again to meet with voters. He cares so much about our state and our district. I know he’ll be an incredible congressman. Thanks, Felix ** Paid for by Adam for Colorado. body:
Mark's Monday Memo
| | | |
---
# Empowering Parents With School Choice: Unleashing the Potential of Education
---
Introduction In today's rapidly evolving world, the education system is crucial in preparing the next generation for success and innovation. However, the one-size-fits-all approach to education fails to meet the unique needs and preferences of individual students. Advocating for school choice is not only about empowering parents but also about recognizing that every child deserves the best possible education tailored to their specific requirements. By allowing parents the freedom to choose the educational institution that best suits their child, we can foster healthy competition, drive performance improvement, and ultimately elevate the quality of education for all.
The Right to Choose As an advocate of school choice, I firmly believe that parents should have the right to decide about their child's education. Just as no two children are identical, no two schools are identical. Parents understand their children's personalities, learning styles, and aspirations better than anyone else. By granting them the right to choose, we acknowledge the vital role of parents as primary stakeholders in their child's education and respect their expertise in making the best choices for their future.
Education Dollars Should Follow the Child In a fair and just society, education dollars should follow the child to the school of their parent's choice. The current system often allocates funds based on a child's zip code, limiting their access to quality education. School choice empowers parents to direct funding to institutions that align with their child's unique needs, preferences, and academic goals. By doing so, we ensure that each child is afforded the same opportunity to succeed, regardless of their socio-economic background.
The Power of Competition Competition is the driving force behind progress and improvement in any field, and education is no exception. When parents are granted the freedom to choose among various schools, a competitive environment is created, motivating educational institutions to continuously enhance their programs, curricula, and teaching methodologies to attract more students.
School Choice Improves Performance Numerous studies have indicated that competition in education leads to improved performance across the board. Let's review some compelling statistics that shed light on the positive impact of school choice:
1\. Academic Achievement: According to a study conducted by the Friedman Foundation for Educational Choice, 31 out of 33 empirical studies found that school choice improves academic outcomes for participating students. They consistently outperformed their peers attending traditional public schools regarding test scores and graduation rates.
2\. Parental Satisfaction: A report by the National Bureau of Economic Research revealed that parents who had the opportunity to choose their child's school reported higher levels of satisfaction and engagement in their child's education.
3\. High School Graduation Rates: In a study published by the Journal of School Choice, it was found that students who attended private schools through voucher programs had significantly higher high school graduation rates compared to their peers in public schools.
4\. Increased Efficiency: An analysis conducted by the CATO Institute reported that states with greater school choice programs tend to demonstrate increased efficiency in education spending, indicating that competition fosters resource optimization.
Conclusion School choice is not about dismantling the public education system but rather about augmenting it with a diverse array of options that cater to the unique needs of individual students. Empowering parents with the freedom to choose the best educational setting for their children allows for competition to thrive, which, in turn, raises the performance bar for all schools. As we advocate for school choice, let us envision a future where every child can access an education that nurtures their full potential, preparing them to become active contributors to society and driving positive change in the world.
As your representative in Congress, I will fight for school choice! Decisions about a child's education should be made as close to that child as possible, preferably by the parents, not some bureaucrat in Washington, DC.
---
Your Questions--Mark's Answers
Mark Robertson answers your questions with direct and honest answers. Here are two questions Mark was asked recently:
Q. Do you support parents' rights by not allowing teachers and schools to give or supply medical advice to students without informing parents and getting their permission? -B.F.. A. I support parents' rights to decide what information their children receive. Parents have primary responsibility for teaching and raising their children. Schools should be there to support parents and teach math, science, reading, and other academic subjects. Schools should not be in the business of indoctrination or social experimentation.
Q. Can you address males competing in female sports, sharing of locker rooms/bathrooms, etc.? I don't want my daughter using the same restroom as a male who decides he is a female and puts on a skirt. -M.S. A. We should protect women's sports. Biological males should not be allowed to compete in women's sports or use women's locker rooms and bathrooms.
Please share this message with friends and neighbors. Have questions for me? Simply reply to this email to ask me a question.
---

Thanks to the generous and kind support from many of you, I am Wheels Up this morning for our Nations Capitol, Washington, DC!
I am humbled by the tremendous support shown by many of you that made it possible for me to buy my ticket last night at 10PM and now be on the way to Washington this morning to attend the Youth Advisory Council Meeting at RNC HQ!
With any luck, those of you that donated will be receiving an exclusive video message from a very important individual and myself thanking you for your support and dedication to the Youth Advisory Council and Gen Z.
While we have not yet met the needed goal for this trip, I was able to make it work and get onboard Southwest Airlines this morning in order to attend the meeting in person.
If you would like to help me reach the goal please consider clicking below to contribute.

I am humbled by the tremendous support shown by many of you that made it possible for me to buy my ticket last night at 10PM and now be on the way to Washington this morning to attend the Youth Advisory Council Meeting at RNC HQ! With any luck, those of you that donated will be receiving an exclusive video message from a very important individual and myself thanking you for your support and dedication to the Youth Advisory Council and Gen Z. I want to tell you a bit about myself and why I’m running for President of the United States. I’m running for President because my parents came to America more than 50 years ago – an America that was strong and proud, and full of opportunities. I want them to know that America again. I’m running for President as a military spouse. My husband is deployed and I want him and his military brothers and sisters to know their sacrifice means something – that we do love this country. I’m running for President for the future of our kids. I have a daughter who just got married and I saw how hard it was for her and her husband to buy a home. I’m doing this for my son who’s a senior in college and shouldn’t have to worry about saying things on a paper that he doesn’t believe in just to get an A. I’m running for my daughter, my son, and our future generations. I want them to experience the freedom and opportunity that the American Dream has to offer. I’m running for President because we have a country to save! I’ve seen that even on our worst days, we are blessed to live in America. But I also know that under Biden’s leadership, times are tough, our future is slipping, and America can be doing so much better. As governor, I was dubbed a “jobs governor” because I slashed our unemployment rate, brought jobs back to our state, and shepherded in a successful economic recovery. At the UN I fought for America, made sure our allies knew we had their backs, stood up for Israel even when others refused, and stood up to murderers and dictators to let them know that America was taking names. I know how to make America strong and proud because I’ve done it before. And I’ll do it as your next President. # Biden's FedNow May Be A Calculated
Attack On Your Freedom
---

In the wake of the controversial Executive Order 14067...

[Pres. Biden has just launched FedNow - a move many are calling the BIGGEST 401(k) heist of the century.](http://trk.secureandprosper.com/c/7/eyJhaSI6NTE5ODk2OTgsImUiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsInJpIjoiMTM4ODM3MTYzNCIsInJxIjoiMDItYjIzMjYxLTYzMDcxYmJkNDkzOTRlNWI4NmViNzM5MGE5ZmRjMDBjIiwicGgiOm51bGwsIm0iOmZhbHNlLCJ1aSI6IjAiLCJ1biI6IiIsInUiOiJodHRwczovL3RyYWNraW5nLmNsaWNrc3RyaXBlMS5jb20vYWZmX2M_b2ZmZXJfaWQ9MTk3NyZhZmZfaWQ9MTA2NSZ1cmxfaWQ9MzkmYWZmX2NsaWNrX2lkPTcwMVJiMDAwMDAwWTRGaUlBSyZzb3VyY2U9ZW1haWwmYWZmX3N1Yj1hY3RpdmVlbmdhZ2VtZW50X25ldHdvcmsmYWZmX3N1YjI9e3RyYW5zYWN0aW9uX2lkfSZhZmZfc3ViMz0wOTAxMjMmYWZmX3N1YjQ9U1VCSUQmYWZmX3N1YjU9ZmVkbm93X2EifQ/EfMQCz14lKa6MtqGXnm3DA)

While America slept, FedNow was silently 'installed' across millions of US bank accounts.

Biden and his scheming cronies want you to believe it's a harmless money transfer network, but insiders know the truth:

["FedNow Is A Trojan Horse For The Digital Dollar."](http://trk.secureandprosper.com/c/7/eyJhaSI6NTE5ODk2OTgsImUiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsInJpIjoiMTM4ODM3MTYzNCIsInJxIjoiMDItYjIzMjYxLTYzMDcxYmJkNDkzOTRlNWI4NmViNzM5MGE5ZmRjMDBjIiwicGgiOm51bGwsIm0iOmZhbHNlLCJ1aSI6IjEiLCJ1biI6IiIsInUiOiJodHRwczovL3RyYWNraW5nLmNsaWNrc3RyaXBlMS5jb20vYWZmX2M_b2ZmZXJfaWQ9MTk3NyZhZmZfaWQ9MTA2NSZ1cmxfaWQ9MzkmYWZmX2NsaWNrX2lkPTcwMVJiMDAwMDAwWTRGaUlBSyZzb3VyY2U9ZW1haWwmYWZmX3N1Yj1hY3RpdmVlbmdhZ2VtZW50X25ldHdvcmsmYWZmX3N1YjI9e3RyYW5zYWN0aW9uX2lkfSZhZmZfc3ViMz0wOTAxMjMmYWZmX3N1YjQ9U1VCSUQmYWZmX3N1YjU9ZmVkbm93X2IifQ/hQaOhE3wzOT5UZwiod7bNQ)

You see...

The FedNow can reroute TRILLIONS of dollars in daily transactions... ... bypassing the traditional banking system and placing your savings directly under the government's microscope.

Marine Corps Capt. Arthur Junior Jackson was a young man in World War II when he singlehandedly blazed a path of destruction through Japanese strongholds on the small island of Peleliu in the Pacific. His calmness under pressure and relentless fighting spirit were a major reason the Allies were able to overtake the island, and they earned him the Medal of Honor.

name: Jenny Beth Martin, Tea Party Patriots Action
subject: Every patriot must read Mark Levin’s new book!
body:
| | |
---
**I am so excited to share our very good friend Mark Levin’s newest book with you:**[**The Democrat Party Hates America**](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.-2Idud0hM1c-xw)** _,_ available tomorrow, Tuesday, September 19th!** You can purchase the book for nearly forty percent off the list price on [**Amazon,**](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.) and in doing so, you can support one of the fiercest American patriots. Mark Levin has been on the side of truth, justice, and the American way of life for decades. We are blessed to have him fighting alongside all of us, and I believe this book should be read by every America-loving patriot, regardless of party.
---
[](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.)
---
| | **Synopsis of _The Democrat Party Hates America_ ...**
In _American Marxism_ , Mark Levin explained how Marxist ideology has invaded our society and culture. In doing so, he exposed the institutions, scholars, and activists leading the revolution. Now, he picks up where he left off: to hold responsible the true malefactors steering our country down the wrong path.

Insightful and hard-hitting as ever, Levin proves that since its establishment, the Democrat Party has set out to rewrite history and destroy the foundation of freedom in America. More than a political party, it is the entity through which Marxism has installed its philosophy and its new revolution.

As in a Thomas Paine pamphlet or a clarion call from Paul Revere, Levin alerts his fellow Americans to the destruction this country is facing, and rallies them to defeat the threat in front of us—more looming than ever. He writes, “Every legal, legitimate, and appropriate tool and method must be employed in the short- and long- run to defeat the Democrat Party. The Democrat Party must be resoundingly conquered in the next election and several elections thereafter, or it will become extremely difficult to undo the damage it is unleashing at breakneck pace.”
---
| | You can [**pre-order the book**](http://lnk.teapartypatriots.-OkGNQ) right now, today, before it is released tomorrow. Let’s help defy the leftists who sit on book review committees and make this book a #1 bestseller! In liberty, Jenny Beth Martin

| Early Voting Starts Friday (Speaker Todd Gilbert)
---
|
---
| It has been an honor representing you in House District 56. I have learned a lot, solved many problems as a team, and call many fellow legislators my friend. Thank you Speaker Todd Gilbert, R-Shenandoah for the shoutout last week in your [statewide budget statement/ press release.](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0014dHiCfk4M4xc6cpavMJlhq_LKxgTwAip2wTLM2UdsEp06OXGg26ZbFipYeMAfv_GEiDWhXy8KcOrBtuoxP3AAcVHnsXRthaWEajbDoYi5BvWLQRtkCyEcoT0QkPw4qEoP7e7g7LF6ugD3rUs1Q35zInxsvyhL7Y0ucCwU24fceolWR5Iw71tj0cym_cmn28zJhoUTGe6yC4dh7V6_cAzBk7P3Hzg4FZcPBEz3HWrJUEd6F38-Nkd_6x7HeCfpSobzNLMAQ_1E2f-HVGD0WwpPw==&c=iX0UTBKG2bEVrIMyHLVLUVz8RF3sm7l08xwp5OQiEy-BLcFLsPzosw==&ch=cg6ldNSsViyGUwOpu4uwvtMtQ0kHyiZdspuahSd-LGTuQPn9mmjyzg==)
I have thoroughly enjoyed fighting to improve Virginia as a Member of the Republican Caucus in the Virginia House of Delegates. Now I am headed to the State Senate. Though we have made progress, we have much work to do to make Virginia the best place to live, work, and raise a family. Destructive and I would even go so far to say "anti-American" Democrat policies to include un-Constitutional mandates have given fellow Virginians countless reasons to vote Republican. I am proud to tell you all fellow Republican legislators in the House and the Senate are united. Isn't it great we have a Republican Governor, Lieutenant Governor, and Attorney General? Let's all work hard, stay on message, and get the win this November so "we" can un-California Virginia!
---
| Congratulations Delegate Rob Bell
---
| [ ](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0014dHiCfk4M4xc6cpavMJlhq_LKxgTwAip2wTLM2UdsEp06OXGg26ZbFipYeMAfv_Gz6v9UYG0THvj52x6Z13fyEgb4kI7udgTuMXpurbGriV7VB_b_ML7lIk-k52Xtz1r0Ut5qIJukBZrDGUQY2NPh8fptXs-2sY35A5BkdfV4KmJsYSpfOSbKjDo0RvQa7TcedPhkxO4UazM109Iowv3q7SCUbEQdfRGkmECXzOq9MtiUCtzLk2xySvptBT1rOyDNCabIvazPZC3IxlhLFI8i7hnOpWM9H1e8Epj_HF1VKuNrgmq9sKNyu8aE9VZXc46_MuYx1SzK4U=&c=iX0UTBKG2bEVrIMyHLVLUVz8RF3sm7l08xwp5OQiEy-BLcFLsPzosw==&ch=cg6ldNSsViyGUwOpu4uwvtMtQ0kHyiZdspuahSd-LGTuQPn9mmjyzg==)

| It has been an honor serving with Delegate Rob Bell. He set a standard of service in the General Assembly few could match. Thank you for being a strong voice, especially for victims of crime. We are excited to hear Rob now works in the AG's office and will continue service to our Commonwealth! 💪🇺🇸🙏 [Click here for the story!](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0014dHiCfk4M4xc6cpavMJlhq_LKxgTwAip2wTLM2UdsEp06OXGg26ZbFipYeMAfv_Gz6v9UYG0THvj52x6Z13fyEgb4kI7udgTuMXpurbGriV7VB_b_ML7lIk-k52Xtz1r0Ut5qIJukBZrDGUQY2NPh8fptXs-2sY35A5BkdfV4KmJsYSpfOSbKjDo0RvQa7TcedPhkxO4UazM109Iowv3q7SCUbEQdfRGkmECXzOq9MtiUCtzLk2xySvptBT1rOyDNCabIvazPZC3IxlhLFI8i7hnOpWM9H1e8Epj_HF1VKuNrgmq9sKNyu8aE9VZXc46_MuYx1SzK4U=&c=iX0UTBKG2bEVrIMyHLVLUVz8RF3sm7l08xwp5OQiEy-BLcFLsPzosw==&ch=cg6ldNSsViyGUwOpu4uwvtMtQ0kHyiZdspuahSd-LGTuQPn9mmjyzg==)

Thanks to you we earned the Republican nomination for State Senate District Ten with one of the largest 4-way convention wins in Virginia history! By putting God first, "We" did this! Now it is time to "SEAL the deal". Let's finish what we started. I am excited to be at the top of the ticket in 10 counties for Senate District 10 this November. My Democrat opponent is campaigning across the district, we are seeing his signs popping up, he is speaking at events, and he is running a write-in campaign against me. Let's not take this for granted, please do everything you can to ensure we get all of your friends and family to vote. God willing, I will win in November and be the first Goochland State Senator in history!

Want a yard sign? Email john@mcguireforvirginia.com

We have a whole lot of Republicans running across Virginia that need our help.

Go to [www.vpap.org](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0014dHiCfk4M4xc6cpavMJlhq_LKxgTwAip2wTLM2UdsEp06OXGg26ZbFipYeMAfv_Gw65dEJ_PaUzFjBHPafrWFCGxRF9jogIvQquhgz5IedPqbWWztt1ZhWBFjFxkWQpOPIE8wKK--ms=&c=iX0UTBKG2bEVrIMyHLVLUVz8RF3sm7l08xwp5OQiEy-BLcFLsPzosw==&ch=cg6ldNSsViyGUwOpu4uwvtMtQ0kHyiZdspuahSd-LGTuQPn9mmjyzg==) and enter your address to find out who's on your ballot, where to vote, and more.
| [ ](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0014dHiCfk4M4xc6cpavMJlhq_LKxgTwAip2wTLM2UdsEp06OXGg26ZbNG-ieorm1Jf_y95TQpH6pf1iNBQi8WvQCSPLr4-_0xTu4ALPOiJeGVAsyNiq-cKAVM0Vr1jRIoHEjXEk_AWbqE4mzbHij9cawx3Gl-ejd47xMYhAPENukRcAhk9fikVAE_ndBCe8NL7&c=iX0UTBKG2bEVrIMyHLVLUVz8RF3sm7l08xwp5OQiEy-BLcFLsPzosw==&ch=cg6ldNSsViyGUwOpu4uwvtMtQ0kHyiZdspuahSd-LGTuQPn9mmjyzg==)
---
| Keep it Positive!
---
| With the right attitude and effort we can win. Right now we are in a very tough General Election and it is going to take everyone working together. It only takes a few people to mess things up. Sadly, we have a few 5th Congrssional District Republicans including a few in leadership publicly taking pot shots at me and acting a fool. Although most 5th members are professional, I wrongly figured those few would end their antics after the convention. This behavior is unbecoming, it is not productive, and it helps Democrats. We can do better. I normally ignore "keyboard warriors" but we are receiving messages of disgust from across the district from constituents (We the People) who want us to win. If you have a concern, let's meet in person and discuss it like adults. Considering the future of our republic is at stake right this very moment, we must do better. Unless you want Democrats to win, your message to Republicans taking public pot shots at fellow Republicans, should be "knock it off". Our focus is on winning the House and the Senate in November. As Governor Glenn Youngkin says, we "must lock arms" because, right now, that is all that matters! Take care, please spread the word, and I look forward to seeing you around the District!
---
